summary: Matched part of src/melee/gr/grdisplay.c, adding grDisplay_801C5B90 and bringing the file report to 50.21% matched, with grDisplay_801C5B90 reported at 544 bytes and 98.93%. The work also added missing declarations needed by the match, including Camera_80030B24, lbShadow_8000EFEC, and a typed StageInfo.x17C callback, and marked prior func_801C5B90 scratch attempts as OK in config/GALE01/scratches.txt.
what_changed: Created the grdisplay.c implementation body from an effectively empty file and added grDisplay_801C5B90.

grDisplay_801C5B90 handles NULL jobj early return, special handling for JObj flag 0x1000, setup of parent/child matrices, inverse concatenation, current camera object lookup, view matrix concatenation, recursive child display traversal, fighter shadow/visibility state updates, lbShadow_8000EFEC, and HSD_JObjDisp.
Changed StageInfo.x17C in src/melee/gr/types.h from void* to bool (*)(Vec3*, int, HSD_JObj*) so grdisplay can call it directly as stage_info.x17C(&sp30, -1, jobj).
Added lbShadow_8000EFEC(void) declaration and address comments to lbshadow.h declarations.
Adjusted lbshadow.h include guard from MELEE_LB_LBSHADOW_H to GALE01_00E9F0 and removed a direct baselib/jobj.h include while relying on forward declarations.
Removed an apparently unnecessary baselib/objalloc.h include from sysdolphin/baselib/displayfunc.h.
Updated config/GALE01/scratches.txt to mark multiple func_801C5B90 scratch entries as OK instead of partial percentages ranging from about 91% to 99.19%.
smart_moves: Recovered a concrete function-pointer type for StageInfo.x17C rather than leaving it as void*, allowing the matched code to express the real stage callback call signature.

Kept grDisplay_801C5B90 close to the low-level engine flow: explicit JObj flag checks, manual matrix temporaries, scoped temporaries, and recursive traversal all mirror patterns likely needed for matching.
Used existing engine APIs instead of stubs: HSD_JObjSetupMatrix, HSD_MtxInverseConcat, HSD_CObjGetCurrent, PSMTXConcat, HSD_JObjDisp, ftLib_800872B0, ftLib_80086644, and lbShadow_8000EFEC.
Added only the missing declarations needed by the new C source, keeping header changes small and targeted.
Cleaned scratch metadata by converting older func_801C5B90 attempts to OK after landing the matched/decompiled version.
decomp_lessons: When a function calls through a global struct member, replacing void* fields with an exact callback prototype can be necessary both for compile correctness and matching readability.

Display functions may depend on cross-system declarations from camera, fighter library, stage info, lb shadow, and HSD baselib; matching them often requires small header recovery across several subsystems.
JObj traversal code can require preserving recursive structure and flag-mask expressions such as jobj->flags & (arg2 << 18) and jobj->flags & (arg2 << 28).
Matrix code may need explicit stack Mtx temporaries and use of HSD_CObjGetCurrent()->view_mtx with PSMTXConcat to reproduce original register/stack behavior.
Anonymous local bitfield struct usage can be a pragmatic way to represent a partially known returned fighter state structure when only one bit, here b4, is needed.
After matching a previously scratched function, update scratches.txt entries to OK so future contributors do not chase obsolete partial attempts.
naming_conventions: Matched stage display function is named grDisplay_801C5B90, following subsystem prefix plus address convention.
Camera functions retain address-style names such as Camera_80030B24 when semantic names are not yet known.
lbshadow functions use lbShadow_8000xxxx names with address comments such as /* 00EFEC */.
Unknown StageInfo fields retain offset names such as x17C until semantics are known, but their C type can still be improved.
Temporary locals in matched code use stack/register-informed names such as sp3C, sp30, temp_r26, and jtmp where semantics are limited.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved a six-element unused u32 stack array in grDisplay_801C5B90, likely to maintain stack layout for matching.
Used scoped temporary variables around HSD_JObjSetupMatrix(jtmp) and PSMTXConcat(tmp, sp3C, sp3C), suggesting deliberate lifetime/register-shaping for the compiler.
Kept the recursive call for the special 0x1000 JObj path as grDisplay_801C5B90(jobj->child, sp3C, arg2, arg3), passing the stack matrix as the display argument.
Used explicit flag constants 0x1000 and 0x10 plus shift-based masks arg2 << 18 and arg2 << 28 rather than replacing them with higher-level abstractions.
Called HSD_ASSERT(82, cobj) immediately after HSD_CObjGetCurrent(), preserving source-line/assert behavior indicated by the decompiled code.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that finish the remaining unmatched grdisplay functions around 0x801C5ABC through 0x801C6288.
Search for later renames or type refinements of StageInfo.x17C to determine its semantic role in stage collision or camera visibility checks.
Check whether the anonymous bitfield returned by ftLib_800872B0 was later named and moved into a real fighter-related struct.
Search for review or follow-up changes involving lbshadow.h include guards, because this PR changed the guard to GALE01_00E9F0.
Inspect later camera decomp PRs for Camera_80030B24 semantics.
